meta_title: For Students: SaaS pricing to Solve no SaaS knowledge (Beginner Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS pricing and overcome no SaaS knowledge. A Beginner-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: SaaS Pricing to Solve No SaaS Knowledge
content.seo_description: Learn how to implement SaaS pricing and overcome no SaaS knowledge. A Beginner-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code for students.
content.answer_block: Overcoming the 'no SaaS knowledge' barrier starts by treating pricing as a technical feature rather than a business mystery. By integrating Stripe via Antigravity and leveraging Claude Code for rapid iteration, you can build a production-ready billing engine in hours, turning your first student project into a scalable SaaS product.
content.intro:
The screen is blank, the documentation feels like a foreign language, and the concept of 'monetization' feels lightyears away from your current coding skills. You have the vision for an app, but the technical debt of 'not knowing how to start' is the only thing standing between you and your first real-world SaaS deployment.

Stop waiting for permission to be an engineer. By treating SaaS pricing as a modular component—just like authentication or database management—you strip away the intimidation. We are going to bridge that knowledge gap using Antigravity and Claude Code to build a robust, revenue-ready pricing infrastructure in a single afternoon.

steps.title: Architecting the Pricing Model
steps.description: Define your tiers before writing code. We focus on the 'Freemium' model to maximize user acquisition for your portfolio project.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a schema for a SaaS pricing model with three tiers: Free, Pro, and Enterprise, including Stripe price IDs and feature flags.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Generate database schema for Stripe-linked subscription tiers'

steps.title: Integrating Stripe via Antigravity
steps.description: Automate the API boilerplate. Antigravity handles the complex Stripe SDK setup so you can focus on the logic.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a Stripe checkout session handler in TypeScript that maps user IDs to subscription metadata.
steps.claude_code_command: claude terminal 'npm install stripe && antigravity init-stripe'

steps.title: Building the Webhook Listener
steps.description: Webhooks are how your app knows when a user pays. This is the heartbeat of your SaaS backend.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write an Express.js webhook endpoint to verify Stripe signatures and update the local database subscription status.
steps.claude_code_command: claude edit server/webhook.ts --prompt 'implement signature verification and database sync'

steps.title: Implementing Middleware Protection
steps.description: Gate your app's features based on the user's subscription status defined in the database.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create an authentication middleware that checks the user's 'subscription_status' flag before allowing access to premium routes.
steps.claude_code_command: claude apply --file middleware/auth.ts --change 'restrict access to premium routes based on subscription status'

steps.title: Testing the Checkout Flow
steps.description: Validate your work using Stripe's test mode to ensure the end-to-end flow works perfectly.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a test script that simulates a user sign-up, checkout completion, and subscription activation.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'execute end-to-end billing integration test'

pro_tips: Always default to Stripe's 'Customer Portal' for billing management to avoid building complex UI for cancellations.
Use environment variables for all Stripe keys; never hardcode your API secrets in the repository.
Implement a 'Grace Period' logic in your database to prevent immediate lockout if a payment fails due to bank processing.
Track subscription events in a logging service like Sentry to debug payment issues before users report them.
Start with a simple 'Buy Now' button rather than a complex dashboard to reduce initial development overhead.
content.next_steps: Once your pricing is live, deploy your app to Vercel or Railway and share your live project link on LinkedIn to build your professional portfolio.
content.conversion_block: You now have the technical blueprint to build a scalable, monetized SaaS application. Don't let your code sit on a local branch; deploy it, iterate, and start collecting real-world feedback. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
